summary: This page introduces the unit step function u(t) as a basic model for causal signals that begin at t = 0. It shows how multiplying a signal by u(t) creates a causal version, as in e^{-at}u(t), and demonstrates how step functions can represent piecewise-defined signals such as a rectangular pulse using delayed steps.

figures.caption: Fig. 1.14 (a) Unit step function u(t) (b) exponential e-atu(t).
Fig. 1.15 Representation of a rectangular pulse by step functions.
